name: Recommended promotions returned
description: Successful POST returning a list of promotion recommendations for a loyalty program member. Mirrors the documented Promotion Recommendations response body (recommendationCount, personalizationId, recommendationList[].rank + promotionDetails, loyaltyProgramName, membershipNumber, loyaltyProgramMemberId).

promotionDetails.name: Summer Bonus Points
promotionDetails.description: Earn double points on summer beverages.
promotionDetails.termsAndCondition: Valid for active members on qualifying summer beverages only.

notes: Resource definition at lines 24552-24664; response schema "Promotion Recommendations" (doc page 604) with full JSON example + property table at lines 32508-32595. Resource path has NO {placeholders}, member/customer is identified via the request body, and count/promotionsPerProductLimit are query params (Resource Example line 24560 shows ?count=3&promotionsPerProductLimit=4). Available version of the resource is 64.0 (line 24563); note the request body 'cart' property table preceding it on page 467 belongs to the prior resource (Cart), not this one. Request body documented fields (lines 24592-24659): personalizationPoint (Required), dataspace (Required, only default data space supported), membershipNumber, loyaltyProgramId (Required when membershipNumber specified), loyaltyProgramMemberId, individualId, eligibleChannel{channel{id,name},retailStore{id,name}}. At least one identifier (membershipNumber+loyaltyProgramId, loyaltyProgramMemberId, or individualId) is required per the cross-conditional Required/Optional notes. recommendationList element type is "Recommendation Details on page 612" (not expanded here), the inline JSON example fully shows its rank + promotionDetails shape, which I used verbatim for field names/casing (productDetails, coupon.couponDetails, isAutomatic, isEnrollmentRequired, isEnrolled, termsAndCondition, etc.). Doc dates use date-only strings (e.g. "2025-03-06") for start/end fields. The doc shows no error-response example for this resource, so fixture 02 is a documented-shape "empty list" success case rather than an HTTP error; IDs are realistic Salesforce-style but invented.
